summary: R2 systems_architect review accepts M4 muster preparation and mobilized force commitments. The prior response authority/provenance blocker is fixed: debtor response advancement and creditor release advancement are now mutually exclusive in sim.record-muster-response validation, release authority is selected only after mixed advancement is rejected, and the previous creditor-fabricates-assembly reproduction now rejects without mutating the commitment. The implementation remains scoped to sim-core/protocol/tests, keeps player and AI on the same GameCommand path, includes canonical hash/runtime validation/query copy coverage, and does not expand into grain consumption, route capacity execution, march execution, siege, battle, postwar settlement, UI, full diplomacy, save-format milestone work, or new dependencies.

tests_run.result: Inspected R2 writer handoff and claimed evidence.

Current branch is feat/m4-muster-prep-commitments. Modified tracked files are limited to packages/protocol/src/index.ts, packages/sim-core/package.json, packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts, packages/sim-core/src/world-state-v0.ts, tests/protocol-command-query-schema.test.ts, and tests/sim-core-m4-campaign-objectives.test.ts. The new tests/sim-core-m4-muster-prep-commitments.test.ts remains untracked and must be staged by the integrator.

tests_run.command: Manual R2 diff inspection of packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts and tests/sim-core-m4-muster-prep-commitments.test.ts
tests_run.result: Verified validateMusterResponsePayload now computes hasDebtorResponseAdvance and hasCreditorReleaseAdvance and rejects mixed advancement before authority selection. Verified tests cover creditor mixed release/assembly fabrication rejection, debtor release authority denial, creditor mixed release/delay/refusal rejection, over-release rejection, and legitimate debtor-response then creditor-release acceptance.

Previously accepted creditor command with assembledTroops 40 and releasedTroops 40 now rejects with code muster-commitment-invalid at payload.commitmentId and message 'Muster response quantities and release quantities must advance in separate commands.' Commitment remains assembledTroops 0, delayedTroops 0, refusedTroops 0, releasedTroops 0, status promised.
Passed. Node and worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd.
Passed architecture boundaries, determinism, root Vitest 27 files / 180 tests, and web package tests 1 file / 3 tests.
Passed Prettier check, ESLint, workspace typecheck, and architecture boundary checks.

tests_run.command: rg scans for Math.random, Date.now, performance.now, new Date, React, Pixi, Electron, and node:fs over touched sim-core/protocol implementation files
tests_run.result: No forbidden authoritative random/time APIs or React/Pixi/Electron/Node fs imports were found in the touched implementation files.

decisions: Accepted R2 fix because response authority is now separated without adding a broader protocol split or scope expansion.
Kept the existing sim.record-muster-response schema as a minimal milestone-appropriate abstraction; validation enforces the debtor-response versus creditor-release modes.
Confirmed the package.json script change only adds the new sim-core muster test file and introduces no dependency changes.
risks: Save-format persistence for M4 muster state remains intentionally out of scope and must be handled by a later M4 save-runtime task before claiming save/load persistence for this state.
The new tests/sim-core-m4-muster-prep-commitments.test.ts is still untracked in the current worktree status and must be staged during integration.
requested_action: Route ACCEPT to lead_orchestrator. M4-MUSTER-PREP-COMMITMENTS-001 may proceed to the next required review/integration step; ensure the untracked muster test file is staged before commit.
